name: Gefitinib
target: EGFR (ERBB1)
mechanism: Reversible ATP-competitive EGFR kinase inhibitor. Binds the ATP pocket, competes with ATP for binding to the kinase domain. Effective in L858R and Exon 19 del but defeated by T790M gatekeeper mutation.
indication: EGFR-mutant NSCLC (L858R, Exon 19 del)
boston_connection: Lynch et al. 2004 (NEJM) — first EGFR mutation paper came from MGH/Dana-Farber, directly enabling gefitinib's clinical success.
note: First EGFR TKI approved. Response rate ~70% in EGFR-mutant NSCLC. PFS ~9–11 months. Now largely superseded by osimertinib first-line.

name: Erlotinib
target: EGFR (ERBB1)
mechanism: Reversible ATP-competitive EGFR kinase inhibitor, same class as gefitinib. Inhibits autophosphorylation and downstream MAPK and PI3K-AKT signaling. Structurally similar to gefitinib with different pharmacokinetic profile.
indication: EGFR-mutant NSCLC; also NSCLC maintenance, pancreatic cancer (combination with gemcitabine)
boston_connection: Paez et al. 2004 (Science) from Dana-Farber co-discovered EGFR mutations in erlotinib responders alongside Lynch et al. at MGH.
note: FDA approved 2004. First-gen TKI with similar efficacy to gefitinib. Also approved in pancreatic cancer. Now largely replaced by osimertinib.

name: Afatinib
target: Pan-HER (EGFR, HER2, HER4) — irreversible covalent
mechanism: Irreversible pan-HER TKI. Forms covalent bond with Cys773 on EGFR (and homologous cysteines on HER2, HER4), blocking all ErbB family signaling. More potent than 1st gen vs some uncommon mutations.
indication: EGFR-mutant NSCLC including uncommon mutations (G719X, S768I, L861Q); also HER2-exon 20 insertions
boston_connection: Clinical trial sites at Brigham and Women's Hospital/Dana-Farber for LUX-Lung trials. Brigham has been an active afatinib enrollment site.
note: Specifically approved for uncommon EGFR mutations. Broader pan-HER activity than 1st gen. PFS advantage vs chemo in LUX-Lung trials.

name: Dacomitinib
target: Pan-HER (EGFR, HER2, HER4) — irreversible covalent
mechanism: Second-generation irreversible pan-HER TKI. Irreversible covalent binding to Cys773 (EGFR), Cys805 (HER2), Cys803 (HER4). Demonstrated improved OS vs gefitinib in ARCHER 1050 trial.
indication: First-line treatment of EGFR-mutant (exon 19 del or L858R) metastatic NSCLC
boston_connection: ARCHER 1050 trial included Brigham and Women's Hospital as enrollment site. Pan-HER mechanism informed by Harvard Medical School structural biology work.
note: ARCHER 1050: improved OS vs gefitinib (34.1 vs 26.8 months). Dose reductions common due to diarrhea/rash. Now less used due to osimertinib preference.

name: Osimertinib
target: Mutant EGFR (L858R, T790M) — irreversible covalent at C797
mechanism: Third-generation mutant-selective EGFR TKI. Designed to spare wild-type EGFR while irreversibly binding mutant (T790M-containing) EGFR via covalent bond with Cys797. Excellent CNS penetration (crosses blood-brain barrier).
indication: EGFR-mutant NSCLC (first-line, T790M+ after prior TKI, adjuvant post-resection)
institution: Dana-Farber Cancer Institute, MGH, Brigham and Women's Hospital
boston_connection: FLAURA trial involvement at Dana-Farber/Brigham. T790M gatekeeper mutation that osimertinib overcomes was characterized at MGH. C797S resistance mechanism studied at MGH and Dana-Farber.
note: Global standard of care first-line. FLAURA: OS 38.6 vs 31.8 months. LAURA: adjuvant benefit. C797S and MET amplification are primary resistance mechanisms.

name: Lazertinib
target: Mutant EGFR — 3rd-gen irreversible TKI (mutant-selective, T790M-active)
mechanism: Third-generation mutant-selective irreversible EGFR TKI structurally similar to osimertinib. Excellent CNS penetration. In combination with amivantamab (MARIPOSA trial), shows improved PFS vs osimertinib monotherapy (23.7 vs 16.6 months).
indication: EGFR-mutant NSCLC first-line in combination with amivantamab (MARIPOSA trial)
boston_connection: MARIPOSA trial enrollment at Dana-Farber. The amivantamab + lazertinib combination strategy was studied at Boston-area sites to address osimertinib resistance.
note: Approved 2024 as part of lazertinib + amivantamab combination. MARIPOSA: PFS 23.7 vs 16.6 months vs osimertinib. Represents shift toward upfront combination to delay resistance.

name: Aumolertinib
target: Mutant EGFR (L858R, T790M) — 3rd-gen irreversible covalent
mechanism: Third-generation mutant-selective EGFR TKI with covalent binding mechanism similar to osimertinib. Designed with optimized selectivity for mutant vs wild-type EGFR. CNS penetration demonstrated in preclinical models.
indication: T790M-mutant NSCLC after prior EGFR TKI failure; first-line EGFR-mutant NSCLC (China)
note: Approved in China 2020 for T790M+ disease; expanded to first-line 2021. AENEAS trial: PFS 19.3 vs 9.9 months vs gefitinib. Not yet approved in US/EU.

name: Furmonertinib
target: Mutant EGFR (L858R, Exon19del, T790M) — 3rd-gen covalent
mechanism: Third-generation mutant-selective EGFR TKI. Covalent irreversible binding to Cys797. High selectivity for mutant over wild-type EGFR. Evaluated in FURLONG trial vs gefitinib.
indication: First-line EGFR-mutant (Exon19del/L858R) advanced NSCLC (China approval)
note: FURLONG trial: PFS 20.8 vs 11.1 months vs gefitinib. China NMPA approved 2021. Not yet US/EU approved. Part of expanding 3rd-gen TKI landscape.

name: BLU-701
target: EGFR-selective (mutant > wild-type) — next-gen covalent TKI
mechanism: Fourth-generation EGFR-selective TKI designed to spare wild-type EGFR more than current 3rd-gen drugs. Potent against sensitizing mutations (L858R, Exon19del) and resistance variants (C797S in cis with T790M). Part of the WT-sparing strategy to reduce on-target toxicity.
indication: EGFR-mutant NSCLC including C797S resistance mutations (osimertinib-refractory)
boston_connection: Blueprint Medicines is headquartered in Cambridge, MA. Phase 1 trial includes MGH Cancer Center as enrollment site. MIT/Broad Institute structural biology collaboration.
note: Blueprint is a Cambridge, MA-based precision oncology company. BLU-701 targets the unmet need after osimertinib failure via C797S. Interim Phase 1 data presented at ASCO 2023.

name: Mobocertinib
target: EGFR exon 20 insertions — selective small-molecule TKI
mechanism: Small-molecule TKI selectively designed for EGFR exon 20 insertion variants. Covalent irreversible binding to Cys797. Designed to fit the altered ATP pocket geometry created by exon 20 insertion loop.
indication: NSCLC with EGFR exon 20 insertions (post-platinum chemotherapy)
boston_connection: Exon 20 insertion biology studied at MGH and Dana-Farber. Trial enrollment at both Boston sites. Post-withdrawal, exon 20 research continues at MGH with focus on amivantamab and next-gen approaches.
note: FDA accelerated approval Sep 2021. Voluntarily withdrawn by Takeda Sep 2023 after EXCLAIM-2 confirmatory trial failed to demonstrate superior benefit over platinum-based chemotherapy.

name: Poziotinib
target: EGFR/HER2 exon 20 insertions — pan-HER irreversible TKI
mechanism: Irreversible pan-HER TKI with a smaller molecular structure optimized to access the sterically-hindered ATP pocket of exon 20 insertion EGFR. Smaller size allows binding despite the insertion loop that blocks larger TKIs.
indication: NSCLC with EGFR or HER2 exon 20 insertion mutations
boston_connection: Phase 2 ZENITH20 trial enrollment at MGH and Dana-Farber. Exon 20 structural rationale developed in part through collaboration with Boston-area academic centers.
note: ZENITH20 cohort 1 ORR 14.8% — modest activity. Development challenged by narrow therapeutic window (skin/GI toxicity). NDA submission did not receive approval; development ongoing.

name: Sunvozertinib
target: EGFR exon 20 insertions — selective covalent TKI
mechanism: Highly selective covalent TKI designed specifically for EGFR exon 20 insertion variants. WU-KONG6 trial demonstrated 60.8% ORR — highest reported for an exon 20 TKI. Optimized selectivity reduces wild-type EGFR toxicity.
indication: NSCLC with EGFR exon 20 insertion mutations (post-platinum chemotherapy)
boston_connection: AstraZeneca partnership brings this to Dana-Farber trial network. Phase 2 WU-KONG6 data presented at ASCO/ESMO with Boston site participation.
note: WU-KONG6: ORR 60.8% (highest among exon 20 TKIs). AZ acquired Dizal 2022. BLA submitted in China. NDA under FDA review. Most promising exon 20-specific TKI in the pipeline.

name: Zipalertinib
target: EGFR exon 20 insertions — selective covalent TKI
mechanism: Oral irreversible covalent EGFR TKI with high selectivity for exon 20 insertion variants vs wild-type EGFR. Phase 1/2 data: ORR 41% at recommended Phase 2 dose. Improved GI/skin tolerability profile.
indication: NSCLC with EGFR exon 20 insertion mutations (previously treated)
boston_connection: Cullinan Oncology is a Cambridge, MA-based company. Lead investigator network centered at MGH Cancer Center. Phase 1/2 dose escalation included MGH as primary site.
note: Cullinan is Cambridge, MA-based. Phase 1/2 ORR 41% at RP2D. Better tolerability than poziotinib. FDA breakthrough therapy designation. Phase 2 expansion ongoing as of 2024.

name: Amivantamab
target: EGFR (extracellular domain) + MET — bispecific IgG1 antibody
mechanism: Bispecific antibody targeting the extracellular domain of both EGFR and MET. Triple mechanism: (1) receptor blockade — prevents ligand binding; (2) ADCC — recruits NK cells via Fc region; (3) trogocytosis — strips EGFR/MET from tumor cell surface. Does not require ATP pocket access.
indication: NSCLC with EGFR exon 20 insertion mutations; also first-line in combination with lazertinib (MARIPOSA)
boston_connection: CHRYSALIS trial (exon 20 approval) and MARIPOSA trial (combination) both enrolled at Dana-Farber and MGH. The MET co-targeting strategy addresses a key osimertinib resistance mechanism studied at MGH.
note: First approved therapy for exon 20 insertions. Also approved with lazertinib first-line. IV administration (vs oral TKIs). PAPILLON trial: amivantamab + chemo vs chemo in exon 20 insertions.

name: CFT8919
target: EGFR L858R (+ compound mutants T790M, C797S) — PROTAC/BiDAC protein degrader
mechanism: First oral allosteric mutant-selective EGFR PROTAC (BiDAC: Bifunctional Degradation Activating Compound). Binds L858R-mutant EGFR allosterically; recruits cereblon (CRBN) E3 ligase; induces ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation of mutant EGFR. Sub-stoichiometric/catalytic — one molecule drives degradation of many target proteins. Reported to degrade L858R/T790M, L858R/C797S, and triple mutant L858R/T790M/C797S.
indication: EGFR L858R-mutant NSCLC including osimertinib-resistant (C797S) disease
boston_connection: C4 Therapeutics is headquartered in Watertown, MA. Founded 2016 with technology from Dana-Farber Cancer Institute and Harvard Medical School. CFT8919 Phase 1 trial at Dana-Farber.
note: C4 Therapeutics is Watertown, MA-based, spun out of Dana-Farber/Harvard. Phase 1 NCT06641609 (~166 planned). C797S prevents osimertinib covalent binding — but a PROTAC degrades via ubiquitin regardless of the covalent site.

name: BT8009
target: Nectin-4 (tumor-expressed) / EGFR pathway — bicycle-toxin conjugate
mechanism: Bicycle Toxin Conjugate (BTC): a constrained bicyclic peptide targeting Nectin-4 (overexpressed in EGFR-mutant NSCLC) conjugated to a cytotoxic payload. Combines high tumor selectivity of the bicyclic peptide scaffold with potent cytotoxic payload delivery. Distinct from antibody-based ADCs — smaller molecular size enables better tumor penetration.
indication: EGFR-mutant NSCLC (Nectin-4 expressing); bladder cancer
boston_connection: Bicycle Therapeutics US operations based in Lexington, MA. Phase 1/2 trial sites include MGH Cancer Center. Nectin-4 expression in EGFR-mutant NSCLC studied at Boston academic centers.
note: Bicycle Therapeutics is Lexington, MA-based. BT8009 differs from classical PROTACs — it's a bicycle-toxin conjugate (BTC). Early Phase 1/2 data shows manageable safety. Novel scaffold class.

name: Patritumab deruxtecan
target: HER3 (ERBB3) — antibody-drug conjugate with DXd topoisomerase I inhibitor payload
mechanism: Anti-HER3 antibody conjugated to DXd (deruxtecan) topoisomerase I inhibitor payload via stable tetrapeptide-based cleavable linker. HER3 is upregulated in EGFR-mutant NSCLC after TKI resistance. Delivers cytotoxic payload selectively to HER3-expressing tumor cells. Bystander effect kills neighboring HER3-negative cells.
indication: EGFR-mutant NSCLC after EGFR TKI (osimertinib) and platinum-based chemotherapy
institution: Dana-Farber Cancer Institute, Brigham and Women's Hospital
boston_connection: HERTHENA-Lung01 (Phase 3) trial enrollment at Dana-Farber/Brigham. HER3 as resistance mechanism in EGFR-mutant NSCLC characterized in part at Dana-Farber. AZ partnership brings this to Boston trial network.
note: HERTHENA-Lung01 Phase 3 vs docetaxel in post-osimertinib NSCLC. Phase 2 ORR 39.0%. HER3-DXd addresses post-TKI unmet need. Represents ADC strategy exploiting HER3 upregulation in TKI-resistant disease.

name: MEDI-5752
target: PD-1 + EGFR — bispecific antibody
mechanism: Bispecific antibody targeting both PD-1 (immune checkpoint) and EGFR (tumor driver). Designed to co-engage T cells (via PD-1 blockade) and tumor cells (via EGFR binding) to redirect immune killing. Addresses the immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment in EGFR-mutant NSCLC, where PD-L1 inhibitors alone are less effective.
indication: EGFR-mutant or EGFR-overexpressing advanced solid tumors, NSCLC
boston_connection: AstraZeneca trials routinely enroll at Dana-Farber. EGFR-mutant NSCLC immuno-oncology combination rationale studied at Dana-Farber and Brigham. MEDI-5752 trial sites include Dana-Farber.
note: Phase 1 dose-escalation. Early data: ORR 26% in EGFR-mutant NSCLC. Bispecific checkpoint-EGFR approach distinct from TKIs — immunological mechanism. Development ongoing.

name: Tepotinib
target: MET kinase — selective type Ib inhibitor
mechanism: Highly selective MET kinase inhibitor targeting the ATP pocket of MET. Used in combination with EGFR TKIs (osimertinib) to address MET amplification-driven resistance — the most common on-target resistance mechanism to 3rd-gen EGFR TKIs (~15–20% of cases). Dual blockade prevents escape via the MET bypass pathway.
indication: METex14-skipping NSCLC; MET-amplified NSCLC (EGFR TKI resistance combination strategy)
boston_connection: MET amplification as EGFR TKI resistance mechanism characterized at MGH by Engelman et al. Tepotinib + osimertinib combination studied at MGH and Dana-Farber in INSIGHT 2 trial.
note: VISION trial: ORR 43.5% in METex14-skipping NSCLC. INSIGHT 2: tepotinib + osimertinib in MET-amplified osimertinib-resistant disease. MET amplification affects ~15–20% of osimertinib-resistant tumors.

name: Capmatinib
target: MET kinase — selective type Ib inhibitor
mechanism: Selective oral MET kinase inhibitor (type Ib). Addresses MET amplification and METex14 skipping as mechanisms of primary or acquired resistance to EGFR TKIs. In combination with osimertinib, can re-sensitize MET-amplified, osimertinib-resistant tumors. Also active as monotherapy in METex14 NSCLC.
indication: METex14-skipping NSCLC; MET-amplified NSCLC (combination strategy with EGFR TKI for resistance)
institution: MGH Cancer Center, Dana-Farber Cancer Institute
boston_connection: MET bypass mechanism research at MGH and Dana-Farber underpinned clinical rationale. GEOMETRY mono-1 trial sites included Dana-Farber. Combination capmatinib + osimertinib studies at MGH.
note: GEOMETRY mono-1: ORR 68% in treatment-naive METex14 NSCLC. ORCHARD trial: capmatinib arm for MET-amplified post-osimertinib disease. Addresses major resistance pathway.
